sections.paragraph:
Κοδράτος, ου, ὁ Quadratus (Epict. 3, 23, 23; Ael. Aristid. 47, 22 K.=23 p. 451 D.; Herodian; OGI 683, 5; Jos., Bell. 2, 241) a proconsul MPol 21; s. Στάτιος. Name of the apologist, abbr. Qua in this lexicon (cp. Eus., HE 4, 3, 1f).

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

abbr. abbr. = abbreviation

Qua Qua = Quadratus fragment, II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 550
